home_subtitle: Estimate the cost of establishing and running a genomic surveillance laboratory
home_based_on: Based on the WHO Genomics Costing Tool (second edition). Walk through 7 guided steps to estimate your annual costs.
step1_desc: Define the pathogen you are sequencing and your expected annual throughput.
step2_desc: Choose your sequencer(s), reagent kit and library preparation approach.
field_kit_price: Reagent kit price (USD) — override if needed
step3_desc: Edit quantity per sample and unit cost. Toggle items on/off.
note_placeholder_costs: Unit costs are placeholders ($5) — enter your actual local prices. Qty/sample is the number of units consumed per sample processed.
step4_desc: For each item, indicate if you need to buy it, already have it, or will skip it. Costs are amortised over the item's lifespan.
step5_desc: Enter annual salary, percentage of time attributed to sequencing, and training costs.
field_exchange_rate: Exchange rate to local currency (1 USD = ?)
note_usd_default: Set to 1 to keep everything in USD.
about_what_body: An independent, open-source web app for estimating genomic surveillance laboratory costs. It implements the same calculation methodology as the WHO Genomics Costing Tool, 2nd edition (2026) — but is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or produced by the World Health Organization. The original tool is a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet; this version provides the same calculations through a guided, mobile-friendly interface.
about_purpose_body: The tool estimates the cost of establishing and running a genomic surveillance laboratory, including sequencing platforms, reagents, equipment, personnel, facility, bioinformatics, and quality management. It is intended for policymakers, lab leaders, health economists, and donor institutions planning or scaling genomic surveillance programmes.
about_how_body1: The tool walks you through 7 steps: lab setup, sequencing platform, consumables, equipment, personnel, facility and bioinformatics, and results. All calculations run in your browser — no data is sent to any server.
about_how_body2: The samples-per-run calculation follows the methodology in Annex 2 of the WHO user manual: reads per sample are derived from genome size × coverage, compared against pathogen-type minimum read thresholds, buffered for off-target reads, and constrained by the barcoding limit of the selected library prep kit.
about_limits_1: Reagent and equipment prices are reference prices from the WHO Excel (2026) and may not reflect local pricing.
about_limits_2: The tool supports up to two sequencing platforms simultaneously.
about_limits_3: Library kit compatibility is not automatically validated — consult manufacturer documentation.
about_limits_4: Establishment cost excludes personnel, reagents, and facility costs.
note_genome_size_edit: You can edit this if your pathogen has a non-standard genome size.
note_capture_all_mode: Capture-all mode: genome size and coverage are not used. Set the minimum reads per sample in Step 2.
step6_desc: Overhead costs, sample transport and your bioinformatics approach.
note_min_reads_usage: Used instead of genome size × coverage for multi-pathogen capture panels.
label_establishment_cost_desc: Capital equipment to be purchased before operations begin
step7_desc: View your annual cost estimate, per-sample cost and export results.
note_coverage_help: Minimum number of times each genome position must be sequenced.
note_buffer_pct_help: Reserve capacity for off-target or low-quality reads — reduces effective samples per run.
note_retest_pct_help: Expected proportion of samples that will need to be re-sequenced.
label_price_editor_desc: Edit prices for equipment, consumables and personnel. Save applies to the current project.
note_price_csv_tip: Download template → edit in Excel → import back
tooltip_pathogen_type: Filters the pathogen drop-down and sets defaults for coverage and library prep. Select Viral, Bacterial, or a capture-all option for panels that simultaneously sequence multiple targets using probe-capture technology.
tooltip_pathogen_name: Auto-populates genome size, coverage and library prep kit options. Select 'Other bacteria' or 'Other virus' if your pathogen is not listed — then edit the genome size manually.
tooltip_genome_size: Used to calculate the maximum number of samples per run. For whole-genome sequencing, enter the full genome size. For targeted NGS, enter the cumulative size of the targeted regions only.
tooltip_samples_per_year: Total annual number of samples to sequence for this pathogen. Used to calculate total annual cost, cost per sample and reagent kit quantities.
tooltip_coverage: Minimum number of times each genome position must be sequenced. Pre-filled per pathogen; adjust if needed. Combined with genome size to calculate the reads required per sample.
tooltip_buffer_pct: Percentage of flow cell capacity reserved for off-target or low-quality reads. Default: 20% for viruses, 30% for bacteria, 0% for capture-all. Reduces the effective number of samples per run.
tooltip_retest_pct: Expected percentage of samples requiring re-sequencing due to quality failure. Enter 0 if the lab does not retest. Increases total reagent and consumable costs.
tooltip_controls_per_run: Number of positive and/or negative controls included per sequencing run. Controls are strongly recommended for QA. They are subtracted from the available patient sample slots per run.
tooltip_lib_prep_kit: Determines the barcoding limit — the maximum number of uniquely indexed samples that can be pooled per flow cell. Ensure compatibility with your sequencing platform.
tooltip_enrichment: Select if an enrichment step (amplicon-based or hybrid capture) is performed in addition to the library prep kit. Additional reagent rows will appear in the Consumables step.
tooltip_min_reads: Minimum number of reads required per sample. Used instead of genome size × coverage for multi-pathogen capture panels where a fixed read depth per sample is specified.
tooltip_unit_cost_equip: Reference prices from the WHO 2026 data. Update to reflect your actual local procurement costs. Customs clearance and import duties can be entered separately in Step 6.
tooltip_life_yr: Expected equipment lifespan in years. Annual cost = total purchase cost ÷ lifespan. Update if the manufacturer recommendation or your lab's asset policy differs.
tooltip_annual_salary: Total annual employment cost including salary and all benefits. Enter the full amount even if this person works only part-time on sequencing — the % time field will pro-rate the cost.
tooltip_pct_time: Percentage of this person's time spent on sequencing the selected pathogen(s). E.g. if 30% of their time is sequencing, enter 30. Annual attributed cost = salary × (% time ÷ 100).
tooltip_training_cost: Annual cost of NGS wet-lab and bioinformatics training for this staff member. Included in total personnel cost but reported as a separate line in the results.
tooltip_facility_monthly: Monthly costs of facility services: rent/lease, maintenance, utilities (electricity, water, gas, internet), waste management, generator and ventilation. Include all services that support the sequencing lab.
tooltip_facility_pct: Percentage of the facility attributed to sequencing. E.g. if the lab processes 10,000 tests per year and 1,200 are sequencing tests, enter 12.
tooltip_transport_cost: Annual cost of sample logistics including shipment, packaging, insurance, customs clearance and export/import fees.
tooltip_bioinformatics: Cloud: software platforms and storage (e.g. Illumina BaseSpace, Terra.bio). In-house: local servers and workstations. Hybrid: a combination of both. Include software licences, storage costs and maintenance.
chart_cost_per_sample_by_workflow: Cost per sample — by workflow step
chart_total_annual_by_workflow: Total annual cost — by workflow step
chart_throughput_curve: Cost per sample vs. annual throughput
label_disclaimer_text: This report was generated using the Genomics Costing Tool, an independent open-source implementation of the WHO Genomics Costing Tool methodology (2nd edition, 2026). All costs are estimates based on user-entered data and reference prices. Actual costs will vary by country, supplier, and institutional context. This tool is not affiliated with or endorsed by the World Health Organization.
